patch.value: pm comments/notes/learnings --add HTML-escapes angle brackets in stored text

Observed 2026-05-28: passing 'pm comments pm-XXX --add "... pm create <type> ..."' stores the text with literal HTML entities <type> instead of preserving < and >. Confirmed on this session's pm-edge/pm-fl0c/pm-th6y comments. Likely a defensive escape applied at append/comment serialization; pm does not render comments as HTML so the escape is unnecessary and harms readability when agents quote shell snippets like 'pm create <type> <title>'. Investigate where the escape happens (probably in core/store/append or a sanitizer hook), and switch to either no-op or a code-fence wrap. Low priority but visible footgun.

Added defensive HTML-entity decode (5 core entities) at the MCP server boundary in src/mcp/server.ts:792. Decodes < > " ' & only when < or > is present in the input (no-op for normal text). Single-pass via lookup map so &lt; stays as literal < (preserves intentional double-encoding). Walker handles nested options/arrays. Pure helper in src/core/shared/html-entity-decode.ts with 100% test coverage.
pm comments/notes/learnings/create/update --add via MCP store text verbatim with literal angle brackets preserved, matching the direct-CLI behavior.

Verified 19 unit tests pass (idempotent, no double-decode, deep nesting, non-string passthrough). Direct CLI continues to pass <type> verbatim. MCP wiring in src/mcp/server.ts:792 ensures every tool-call argument tree is normalized before reaching any handler.
Shipped defensive HTML-entity decode at MCP server boundary.
